_comment: glasse accessibility functions for the Deepgram Voice Agent. These are CLIENT-SIDE functions: when the agent calls one, Deepgram sends your app a FunctionCallRequest over the websocket; the app executes it (camera->Claude, captions, memory, etc.) and replies with a FunctionCallResponse. Reuse the app's existing dispatchTool logic. owner_id for memory is supplied by the app from auth, not by the model. Goes in agent.think.functions[] of the Settings message (or add each one in the console).

functions.name: describe_scene
functions.description: Describe what the glasses camera currently sees, for a blind or low-vision user. Use for 'what's in front of me', 'describe this', 'take a picture and describe it'. Brief by default; pass detail='detailed' when the user asks to be descriptive or wants more.

detail.description: brief = 1-2 sentences (default); detailed when the user asks for more.

question.description: Optional specific question about the scene, e.g. 'what color is the mug?' or 'is there a free seat?'

functions.name: read_text
functions.description: Read or translate any text the camera sees, aloud and verbatim: signs, labels, mail, menus, receipts, screens, and braille. Use for 'read this', 'what does this say', 'translate this braille'.

question.description: Optional instruction, e.g. 'translate to English', 'just the prices', 'read the whole menu'.

functions.name: identify_objects
functions.description: Start or stop continuous, on-device naming of objects ahead (a running 'what's around me'). Use 'start' for 'tell me what's around', 'stop' to end it.

action.description: start or stop continuous object identification.

functions.name: start_captions
functions.description: Turn on live speech-to-text captions on the lens for a deaf or hard-of-hearing user. Use for 'turn on captions', 'what are they saying', 'caption this conversation'.

functions.name: read_fingerspelling
functions.description: Open the Sign reader, which reads a signer's fingerspelled letters from the camera onto the lens. Use for 'read fingerspelling', 'sign language mode', 'read signing'.

functions.name: navigate
functions.description: Give walking directions to a place. Use for 'take me to', 'how do I get to', 'directions to'. Returns spoken, step-by-step guidance.

destination.description: Where the user wants to go (address or place name).

functions.name: call_for_help
functions.description: Phone the user's saved emergency contact. Use AS SOON AS the user asks for help or signals an emergency ('call for help', 'I need help', 'emergency') — don't stall or ask extra questions. Don't use casually.

functions.name: remember_preference
functions.description: Store a DURABLE user preference or fact so it applies in future sessions. Call this whenever the user states a lasting preference or routine, e.g. 'always describe in detail', 'I take the bus to work', 'read menus when I'm at a restaurant'. Do NOT call it for one-off requests.

text.description: The memory as a short third-person statement, e.g. 'prefers detailed scene descriptions'.

type.description: What kind of memory this is.

functions.name: recall_preferences
functions.description: Look up what is already known about this user that's relevant to the current request, so you can apply their preferences automatically. Call this at the start of a task when prior context would help (e.g. before describing a scene, to honor their preferred level of detail).

query.description: What you're about to do or the topic, e.g. 'describe scene', 'navigation', 'restaurant'.

functions.name: set_text_to_speech
functions.description: Turn the assistant's spoken voice on or off (the lens still shows text either way). Use for 'turn text to speech off', 'stop talking', 'be quiet', 'speak to me again'.

on.description: true = speak replies aloud; false = stay silent (lens only).
